title: Anyswap - Open App
meta_description: anyswap app route for legacy cross-chain swaps: open the official interface, connect your wallet, choose networks, and verify every transaction.
hero_h2: Open the legacy cross-chain swap interface
hero_lead: anyswap opens a legacy cross-chain swap route for users who want to connect a wallet, choose assets and networks, review the route, and continue inside the official app.
what_is: Anyswap is the legacy cross-chain swap and bridge brand that later became Multichain, a router for Web3 cross-chain interactions according to the Multichain docs. Use this page as an app-first route, then verify the domain before connecting a wallet.

how.d: Use the official app link and check the domain before connecting a wallet.
Select a compatible Web3 wallet and switch to the source network shown in the interface.
Pick the asset, source network, destination network, and amount, then review the displayed route.
Approve only the transactions you understand and keep enough native gas on the source network.

points.p: The page keeps copy minimal and sends swap-intent traffic straight to the interface where routing and wallet actions happen.
Anyswap became part of the Multichain lineage; the Anyswap GitHub lists public repositories for the protocol history.
Check the domain, token, network, destination address, and wallet prompt before approving any transaction.

faq.a: anyswap is the earlier cross-chain swap and bridge brand associated with Multichain; the official docs state that Multichain was born as Anyswap on July 20, 2020.

faq.q: Is anyswap the same as Multichain?
faq.a: Anyswap was the earlier brand, while Multichain became the broader cross-chain router identity. Always verify which app and domain you are using before connecting a wallet.

faq.q: How do I use anyswap safely?
faq.a: Open the official app URL, confirm the domain, connect your wallet, review the selected asset and networks, and approve only the transactions you intend to send.
Yes, swap and bridge flows normally require a compatible Web3 wallet because transaction approvals are signed from your own wallet.
No. This page is an educational route to the app; crypto swaps involve smart-contract, liquidity, bridge, and market risks.

footer_note: Not the official app; verify anyswap-v2.github.io before connecting a wallet. Educational only, not financial advice.
